logline: A radiologist confronts the moment her diagnostic instincts become secondary to an AI that sees what she cannot.
scene: a radiologist sitting alone in a dimly lit reading room, face illuminated by the cool glow of medical imaging monitors showing chest CT scans, her hand paused mid-gesture over the lightboard, golden late-afternoon sun filtering through partially closed blinds behind her, coffee cup casting a long shadow, intimate contemplative moment
keyProps: Medical imaging monitors displaying chest CT scans
A coffee cup casting a long shadow
A lightboard with a paused hand gesture
camera: Eye-level intimate framing in a dimly lit workspace, 50mm lens with shallow depth of field
lighting: Cool monitor glow on the face contrasted with warm golden late-afternoon sun filtering through partially closed blinds
productionDesign: Documentary realism with warm golden tones, subtle film grain, near-future clinical setting that feels grounded and intimate
signatureTwist: extreme close-up of a human eye reflecting the glow of a medical scan, the iris catching warm amber light from a window while the pupil mirrors cool blue-white CT imagery, shallow depth of field with soft bokeh of a clinical setting behind, the moment of recognition captured in the tension between two light sources
